observed: The executable reconciler existed with six deterministic contract tests before this slice.
The aggregate queue and owner-scoped update files are separate historical sources.
A repository-aware generator and a GitHub Actions verification workflow are now committed.
inferred: Generating a derived current view in CI reduces stale aggregate-only reads while preserving source history.
proposed: Consumers should use the generated artifact as a reviewable projection, not as a replacement for ACTIVE_QUEUE.json or queue-update records.
superseded: The prior handoff that repository discovery and CI wiring were absent is superseded by this implementation slice.
unresolved: No completed workflow run or uploaded artifact was available at evidence-recording time.
Commit ancestry is captured per source file, but explicit ancestry comparison between competing commits remains future work.

behavior: binds each source to path, blob SHA, commit SHA, and commit time
fails CI on validation errors or unresolved conflicts
uploads the projection without overwriting source history

verification.source_behavior: Committed source and workflow were accepted by GitHub and can be fetched by immutable commit.
verification.acceptance_limit: Do not claim the generated artifact exists until a workflow run completes and its artifact is inspected.

privacy_boundary: Public repository paths, queue metadata, commit identities, and abstract operational conclusions only. Raw private chats, health or pet-health information, credentials, contact data, and unreviewed uploads are excluded.
falsification_route: Run the workflow against malformed updates, owner mismatches, and equal-time divergent states; acceptance fails if the job passes despite non-empty validation_errors or conflicts.
